blocks.body: Future Perfect Continuous — will have been + V-ing for actions that will still be continuing up to a future moment or event, with a focus on process and duration.

{"level":"B2","intro":"Future Perfect Continuous shows a process or duration that will already have been continuing for some time by a specific future moment or another future event.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the model will have been + V-ing for all subjects.","example":"I will have been working. / They will have been waiting."},{"label":"Duration before a future point","color":"blue","text":"This tense often answers the question how long? before a future limit.","example":"By noon, she will have been studying for three hours."},{"label":"Before another future event","color":"amber","text":"It helps show a process that will still be continuing up to another future event.","example":"By the time you arrive, we will have been driving for six hours."},{"label":"Focus on process","color":"violet","text":"The key point is not only the result, but the process itself or the accumulated duration.","example":"Next month, he will have been working here for ten years."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form and use, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions. Each card includes short rules from the matching lesson.","items":[{"label":"B2","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"will have been + V-ing and the main meanings of Future Perfect Continuous.","rules":[{"label":"Form","formula":"will have been + V-ing","text":"The form is the same for every subject: will have been + V-ing.","example":"I will have been working for ten hours."},{"label":"Use","formula":"duration by future time","text":"Use it for a process continuing up to a specific future moment.","example":"By June, she will have been studying here for a year."}]},{"label":"B2","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"will not have been / won't have been + V-ing for processes that will not continue until that point.","rules":[{"label":"Negative","formula":"won't have been + V-ing","text":"Use will not / won't have been + V-ing for negatives.","example":"They won't have been waiting long."},{"label":"Process absent","formula":"not continuing","text":"This shows that the process will not have continued up to that moment.","example":"He won't have been working there for a year yet."}]},{"label":"B2","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Will you have been working...? / How long will she have been...? / Yes, I will.","rules":[{"label":"Question","formula":"Will + subject + have been + V-ing?","text":"Put Will at the beginning and keep have been before V-ing.","example":"Will you have been working for ten hours?"},{"label":"How long","formula":"How long will...?","text":"How long asks about duration up to a future moment.","example":"How long will she have been studying by June?"}]},{"label":"B2","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"for, by, by the time, by then, how long, all day, all morning, since.","rules":[{"label":"Duration","formula":"for + period","text":"For shows how long the process will continue before a future moment.","example":"By 6, I will have been working for eight hours."},{"label":"Recent activity","formula":"by / by the time","text":"By sets the future point up to which we count the duration.","example":"By the time you arrive, we will have been waiting for an hour."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Up to a future moment","color":"emerald","description":"Show a process that will still be continuing up to a specific time, date, or deadline.","examples":[{"en":"By 6 p.m., I will have been writing for four hours.","ua":"By six o'clock, the process will already have lasted four hours."},{"en":"Next June, they will have been living here for a decade.","ua":"By that future moment, the duration will already have built up."}]},{"label":"Up to another future event","color":"blue","description":"Say that an action will still be continuing before another future event happens.","examples":[{"en":"By the time the meeting starts, we will have been waiting for an hour.","ua":"The waiting will still be continuing up to the start of the meeting."},{"en":"When she lands, he will have been travelling all night.","ua":"The process continues up to another future event."}]},{"label":"Duration and how long","color":"amber","description":"This tense sounds natural when you want to stress the accumulated duration before a future limit.","examples":[{"en":"By then, I will have been learning Polish for two years.","ua":"The focus is on how long the process will have lasted."},{"en":"By Friday, she will have been working on the project for six weeks.","ua":"We stress duration before a deadline."}]},{"label":"Process before a result","color":"violet","description":"The form works well when the important thing is the long process before a future outcome or deadline.","examples":[{"en":"By next month, the team will have been testing the app for six weeks.","ua":"The key idea here is not the finished result, but the testing process itself."},{"en":"By the end of the season, she will have been training every morning for months.","ua":"The focus is on a regular process before a future point."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Future Perfect Continuous = will have been + V-ing.","This tense shows a process or duration that will still be continuing up to a future moment or event.","It often answers the question how long? before a future limit.","If the process matters most, this form often fits better than talking only about a finished result."]}
